name: Scott Hamilton
abstract: Scott Hamilton (born September 12, 1954) is an American jazz tenor saxophonist associated with swing and straight-ahead jazz. His eldest son, Shō Īmura, is the vocalist of the Japanese rock band Okamoto's.

interviews.title: Scott Hamilton interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 23, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: Can you imagine David Letterman saying “our main guest tonight is Scott Hamilton?” Of course not. In the days when I did the show you could.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Rossano Sportiello
about_quotes.evidence: So one time we had this duet concert in Italy. Just me and Scott.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rossano Sportiello interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 2, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Flanigan
about_quotes.evidence: I had this chance to go down and jam and play with Scott Hamilton... So I got all the records that Duke told me to get... and then I would join Scott’s band... we only did six gigs before Scott left for New York
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Flanigan interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: the early part of my tour was working mostly with Scott Hamilton... We worked in St. Paul, Minnesota and then we went to L.A... I’ve got to give Carl Jefferson credit... that was with Jake and Scott.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Vaché
about_quotes.evidence: We’ve just continued the relationship for a long time. ... Scott and I did Rosemary Clooney for a number of years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Vaché interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 23, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I made an effort to distance myself from that influence, from Scott’s influence.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997
